title: The Mirage of China’s Military Edge
summary: Contrary to alarmist predictions, China is not currently capable of conquering Taiwan, nor likely to be anytime soon, as trends in military technology now favor Taiwan and the US while motivating Japan, the Philippines, and others to bolster their capabilities as well.

title: China Makes New US Warship Target for Missile Tests, Images Show
summary: The PRC builds a new missile target in the shape of a U.S. Navy destroyer in the country’s northwestern desert.

title: China's Cai Qi cements spot as Xi confidant as head of Communist Party school
summary: Xi Jinping appointed Cai Qi as head of the Central Party School, replacing Chen Xi and giving the longtime Xi confidant a rare concentration of organizational, doctrinal, and administrative power in a post previously held by both Xi and Hu Jintao before they reached the top leadership.

title: China as an absolute advantage economy
summary: Yasheng Huang provides insight into the puzzle of why China is dominating both high-end exports like cars and labor-intensive exports like t-shirts.

title: China Is Pulling Up the Ladder Behind It How Beijing’s Export Strategy Will Keep Poor Countries Poor.
summary: China is pursuing the unprecedented goal of retaining comparative advantage in nearly all manufacturing and the resulting "China squeeze," amplified by record trade surpluses and currency manipulation, is inflicting its gravest damage on poorer countries, whose development is being foreclosed by losing access to the manufacturing-export path that lifted China itself out of poverty.

title: A bridge to Canada may be blocked by the Trump administration
summary: The Gordie Howe bridge spans the most important border crossing between the U.S. and Canada. President Donald Trump has said he doesn't want it open yet.

title: U.S. and Iran exchange strikes, underscoring the fragility of the ceasefire
summary: President Trump accused Iran of violating their framework agreement, and Tehran threatened to halt negotiations with the U.S. entirely. Meanwhile, violence continued between Israel and Hezbollah.

title: Politics chat: SCOTUS hands Trump a win, GOP diverges on housing bill
summary: The contours of the midterms are coming into focus and there appears to be strategic daylight between the administration and Congressional Republicans on what to run on.

title: These church members disagree on politics. Together they're wiping out medical debt
summary: Trinity Moravian Church, a politically diverse congregation in Winston-Salem, N. C., has been raising money to retire medical debt in the surrounding community.

title: U.S. Rep. Julia Letlow, endorsed by Trump, wins the GOP primary for Senate in Louisiana
summary: Letlow, who was endorsed by Trump, defeated state Treasurer John Fleming in the two-candidate runoff after they finished ahead of Cassidy in the GOP primary May 16.

title: How the Supreme Court decides its cases — a step‑by‑step guide
summary: Grasping how the nation's highest court makes policy requires stepping into an exceptionally regulated and sometimes hidden routine.

summary: President Trump said Sunday that the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool will be drained shortly after the country’s 250th anniversary celebrations wrap up, accusing criminals of both damaging the lining and creating algae. Thom Tillis (N.C.) said Sunday he has a “positive predisposition” toward acting Attorney General Todd Blanche, whom President Trump has nominated for the full-time post. “I'm going to go through the nomination process. I have got a positive predisposition towards Blanche,” Tillis, a member of the Senate Judiciary Committee, told host Jake Tapper...

title: MAHA feels betrayed after Supreme Court ruling on Monsanto, glyphosate
summary: Prominent activists with the "Make America Healthy Again" (MAHA) movement are raging and saying they feel betrayed after the Supreme Court sided with pesticide maker Monsanto on Thursday and said it did not need to put a warning label about a potential cancer risk associated with its Roundup weedkiller. title: Three firefighters die in Colorado blaze
summary: Three firefighters died Saturday in a blaze in western Colorado, Interior Secretary Doug Burgum said Sunday. “Last night, we learned that three wildland firefighters—serving with the U.S. Forest Service and the U.S. Wildland Fire Service—lost their lives on Saturday, June 27, while responding jointly to the Knowles and Gore fires along the Colorado-Utah border,” Burgum...

title: Bill Maher Set to Receive Mark Twain Prize at the Kennedy Center
summary: A frequent critic of President Trump, Mr. Maher will take the stage on Sunday at a time of upheaval at the arts institution that once bore the president’s name.

title: Rats, Leaks and Broken Elevators: Repair Backlog Plagues Federal Buildings
summary: After decades, deferred maintenance totals an estimated $50 billion. But getting repair funds from Congress is a laborious process.

title: White House Secretly Swayed Board Meant to Protect Federal Workers From Unfair Firings
summary: Behind the scenes, the Trump White House went to extensive lengths to advance its theory of executive power, potentially giving the president remarkable leeway to install loyalists at nearly every echelon of government.

title: Four Black women. Nine degrees. Not one steady paycheck.
summary: The president promised to save “Black jobs,” but his policies have resulted in fresh pain for the Black middle class as the employment gap widens.

title: Why the nation’s founding document is marred by stains, a mysterious handprint
summary: The physical journey of the Declaration of Independence tells its own story. Its imperfections and endurance reflect those of the America it helped create.

title: Two boys rescued from Venezuela earthquake rubble after days of being trapped
summary: Rescuers spent six hours carefully digging to reach one of the boys, as many residents continue searching through the rubble by hand, still waiting for heavy machinery to arrive.

title: Europe's heatwave linked to 1,300 deaths, WHO says, as Germany hits record 41.7C
summary: WHO chief Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus warned that Europe is not prepared for high temperatures.

summary: The pilot and 10 passengers - including five first-time parachutists - died in the incident, local officials said.

title: Trump-backed Julia Letlow faces John Fleming in Louisiana GOP Senate runoff
summary: U.S. Rep. Julia Letlow, who was endorsed by Trump, and state Treasurer John Fleming are competing in the runoff. The two finished ahead of Sen. Bill Cassidy in the May 16 primary after Trump denounced the two-term senator, who voted to convict him following his 2021 impeachment.

title: Trump says he is nominating former Oklahoma state trooper Lance Schroyer as ICE director
summary: Schroyer's nomination comes after former ICE director Todd Lyons resigned at the end of May. David Venturella, a former executive at a private prison operator, has been serving as the acting head of the agency.

title: What Is Temporary Protected Status?

summary: Congress passed the Marine Mammal Protection Act (MMPA) in 1972 (P.L. 92-522; 16 U.S.C. §§1361-1423h) to conserve marine mammal populations and protect them from extinction or depletion as a result of human activities. title: Supply Chain, Energy, and AI Nexus
summary: The authors identify the inventory of critical electrical equipment for artificial intelligence (AI) data center operations and the impact of supply chain vulnerabilities on the U.S. power grid’s ability to meet AI demand by 2030.

title: Can LLM Agents Select and Engage with Biological Tools?
summary: Large language model agents are capable of performing initial interactions with biological tools (BTs), indicating that agents could lower expertise barriers for actors seeking to use BTs. Targeted testing of agent design capabilities is warranted.

title: AI agents put offensive cyber within reach of novices
summary: Agentic AI models now enable non-experts to conduct advanced offensive cyberattacks. This study finds AI agents can autonomously breach complex systems rapidly, highlighting urgent needs for evolved defence and better cyber risk assessments.

title: Forced Labor Contracts Threaten America’s Workplace Freedom
summary: Should the federal government step in and impose union contracts when labor negotiations stall? Rep. Donald Norcross, New Jersey Democrat, thinks so. title: From Washington, DC, to the States: Rethinking Head Start
summary: Social science research finds that Head Start, a federally funded preschool program for children from low-income families, does not prepare children for K–12 schools, nor does it improve their social skills. After more than six decades and more than $240 billion in federal spending, Head Start is a poor option...

title: Understanding How Impact Fees Shape Housing Development Feasibility and Local Fiscal Revenue
summary: High impact fees contribute meaningfully to housing production costs in California. A 25-percent reduction enables hundreds of new projects in high-cost cities; increased property and sales tax revenue offsets forgone fees in four to seven years.

title: Cultivating a Warrior Ethos in the Air Force
summary: RAND researchers examined how the U.S. Air Force can cultivate an Airminded Warrior identity, finding that the concept requires more development. They recommend several actions to strengthen this identity’s development in initial training and beyond.
